title: helmet({ hsts: false }) — Strict-Transport-Security is off, allowing HTTPS-downgrade attacks

params.passDetail: helmet keeps HSTS on (hsts is not false) — a Strict-Transport-Security header pins browsers to HTTPS.
params.failDetail: helmet is configured with hsts: false, removing the Strict-Transport-Security header entirely. Browsers are no longer told to use HTTPS-only, so a network attacker can strip TLS and serve the site over plain HTTP (SSL-strip), intercepting credentials and session cookies. Leave HSTS enabled (helmet's default) with a real max-age.
params.absentCallDetail: No helmet() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: helmet() does not set hsts; the default (HSTS enabled) applies.

provenance.note: hsts: false is set to test the site over plain HTTP and left in, reopening the SSL-strip window. Distinct from the maxAge: 0 variant — this removes the header outright.
